{{Shrine | Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naiku Misakadono-no-Kami 2.jpg|250px]]
Naikū-administered shrine: Misakadono-no-Kami | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} {{Shrine | Name = Misakadono | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Misakadononokami-geku.jpg|250px]]
Gekū-administered shrine: Misakadono | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} [[File:Geku Imibiyaden.jpg|thumb|Imibiyaden of Gekū
Misakadono is enshrined beyond this hall, where general visitors are not allowed to enter.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 36.]] '''Misakadono-no-Kami''' (御酒殿神) refers to both the deity and the [[Shinto shrine]] enshrining this deity located in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan. There are two such shrines: one is administered by {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū), and the other by {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū). During the '''Misakadono Festival''', prayers are offered not only for the safe brewing of sacred sake (神酒) at the shrine but also for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry.{{Cite web|lang=ja|author=Radio Cube FM Mie|authorlink=Mie FM Broadcasting|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|title=December 7 Broadcast: Misakadono|date=2013-04-04|accessdate=2013-09-14|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0912-2300-12/www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|archivedate=2013-09-12}}. == Overview == [[File:Sake produced in Mie prefecture.jpg|thumb|Sake from Mie Prefecture offered to the Inner Shrine]] This section describes information common to both shrines. The enshrined deity, Misakado-no-Kami, is the god of the {{ill|Misakado|ja|御酒殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakado|qq=}} (Sake Hall), and the Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine and Outer Shrine are the same deity. In the past, sake was brewed in the Misakado and offered before the gods. However, since brewing began to be conducted at the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|qq=}}, the Misakado is now used to temporarily store sake offered during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as during the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} in October.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 56, 58Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 36, 115 Four types of sake are offered at the Misakado: {{ill|shiroki|ja|白酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shiroki|qq=}} (white sake), {{ill|kuroki|ja|黒酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuroki|qq=}} (black sake), {{ill|reishu|ja|醴酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reishu|qq=}} (sweet sake), and {{ill|seishu|ja|清酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seishu|qq=}} (refined sake). Refined sake is donated by patrons, while the other three types are brewed at the Imibiya-den of the Inner Shrine.Sakurai (1970): p. 88 Additionally, during other rituals, reishu and seishu are offered, and for the {{ill|Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|ja|日別朝夕大御饌祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|qq=}}, only seishu is offered at the Misakado. In ancient times, the Misakado brewed two types of sake: '''Honashi Kiyosake''' (火無浄酒), made simply by adding water drawn from the sacred well of {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} to crushed rice called {{ill|shitogi|ja|粢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shitogi|qq=}}, and '''Homuke no Kanmiki''' (火向御酒), a type of {{ill|amazake|ja|甘酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/amazake|qq=}}.Sakurai (1970): p. 87 === Misakado Festival === The '''Misakado Festival''' (御酒殿祭) is held three times a year in June, October, and December. The festival prays for the successful brewing of sake offered to the gods and for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry, with {{ill|kōji|ja|糀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kōji|qq=}} (rice malt) being offered to the Misakado. During the festival held on September 25, 2013 (Heisei 25), 38 sake breweries from the Mie Prefecture Sake Brewers Association offered 38 4-to barrels of sake to the Inner Shrine.Yoshiko Kawaharada, "High-Quality Sake Offered for Prosperity of Brewing Industry," *Chunichi Shimbun*, September 26, 2013, Mie Edition, p. 20 == Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Misakado-no-Kami, under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine, is located to the left of the {{ill|Yuki-no-Mikura|ja|由貴御倉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Mikura|qq=}}, north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=}} in the Inner Shrine precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115 It is Ranked 5th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15–16 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16 Until the early modern period, white sake was brewed at the Imibiya-den by the {{ill|Sakatoku Monoimi|ja|酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatoku Monoimi|qq=}} and black sake was brewed at the purification site by the {{ill|Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|ja|清酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|qq=}}.Sakurai (1970): pp. 155–156 Black sake was brewed using water from the clear stream of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}}.Sakurai (1970): p. 156 Since the Meiji era, priests have been responsible for brewing both white and black sake.Katō (1979): p. 284 According to the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Inner Shrine measured 40 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaku|qq=}} (approximately 12 meters) in length, 17 shaku (approximately 5.1 meters) in width, and 8 shaku (approximately 2.4 meters) in height.Katō (1982): p. 807 == Misakado of the Outer Shrine == The Misakado of the Outer Shrine is located behind the Imibiya-den along the {{ill|Kita-gomon Sando|ja|北御門参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-gomon Sando|qq=}} within the Outer Shrine precinct. Since it is in a restricted area inaccessible to the general public, it is worshipped remotely from the front of the Imibiya-den.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 58 It Ranks first among the four shrines under the jurisdiction of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15, 17 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17 Although the Misakado of the Outer Shrine now stands quietly, until the early modern period, there was an administrative office next to it, along with sacred halls and three kitchens, where {{ill|naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naorai|qq=}} (sacred feasts) were held in the front courtyard. It was an important site for rituals of the Outer Shrine.Sakurai (1970): pp. 88–90 According to the ''Toyuke Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Outer Shrine measured 25 shaku (approximately 7.5 meters) in length, 16 shaku (approximately 4.8 meters) in width, and 9 shaku (approximately 2.7 meters) in height. == Sake Brewing at the Shrine == According to a paper by Hyakuichi Katō, the sake brewed for the 1977 (Shōwa 52) Kannamesai was prepared using 25 kg of white rice harvested from the sacred fields in Kusube-chō, Ise City, 2.7 kg of rice malt purchased from a supplier in {{ill|Yokkaichi|ja|四日市市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi|qq=}}, and 30 liters of water from the sacred well of Kami-no-Mii Shrine. It was brewed as {{ill|doburoku|ja|どぶろく|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/doburoku|qq=}} (unrefined sake) without a starter mash. Twelve days after brewing, the sake was divided into two: the plain batch became white sake, while ash from plants was added to create black sake. The total amount of sake brewed in 1977 was 180 liters. Although white and black sake are technically cloudy sake, they are classified as "refined sake" under Japanese law due to filtration. Reishu contains less than 1% alcohol, so it is not legally considered "sake." == Access == ; Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * Approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-shi Station|qq=}} (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}}. Get off at ''Naikū-mae''.Mie Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Travel Guide]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} via Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Omikemichi) from Ise-nishi IC on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}} or via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 23|qq=}} from Ise IC. Parking available nearby. ; Misakado of the Outer Shrine * Approximately a 5-minute walk from the south exit of Ise-shi Station (JR side) via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from the ''Ise-shi Ekimae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * Directly in front of the ''Gekū-mae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * About 5 minutes north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway. Parking for up to 448 vehicles is available nearby.Jingū Administration, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 加藤百一（1979）"清酒を造る神社"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''74'''(5):282-289. * 加藤百一（1982）"酒神と神社（12）"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''77'''(11):806-810. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 200px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area08/area08_14.jpg 御酒殿神（皇大神宮所管社）] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_25.jpg 御酒殿（豊受大神宮所管社）] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0116.html 御酒殿神（みさかどののかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0207.html 御酒殿（みさかどの） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御酒殿神|version=102708708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sake brewing in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御酒殿神]] [[sn:Misakadono-no-Kami]]

{{Shrine | Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naiku Misakadono-no-Kami 2.jpg|250px]]
Naikū-administered shrine: Misakadono-no-Kami | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} {{Shrine | Name = Misakadono | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Misakadononokami-geku.jpg|250px]]
Gekū-administered shrine: Misakadono | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} [[File:Geku Imibiyaden.jpg|thumb|Imibiyaden of Gekū
Misakadono is enshrined beyond this hall, where general visitors are not allowed to enter.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 36.]] '''Misakadono-no-Kami''' (御酒殿神) refers to both the deity and the [[Shinto shrine]] enshrining this deity located in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan. There are two such shrines: one is administered by {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū), and the other by {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū). During the '''Misakadono Festival''', prayers are offered not only for the safe brewing of sacred sake (神酒) at the shrine but also for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry.{{Cite web|lang=ja|author=Radio Cube FM Mie|authorlink=Mie FM Broadcasting|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|title=December 7 Broadcast: Misakadono|date=2013-04-04|accessdate=2013-09-14|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0912-2300-12/www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|archivedate=2013-09-12}}. == Overview == [[File:Sake produced in Mie prefecture.jpg|thumb|Sake from Mie Prefecture offered to the Inner Shrine]] This section describes information common to both shrines. The enshrined deity, Misakado-no-Kami, is the god of the {{ill|Misakado|ja|御酒殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakado|qq=}} (Sake Hall), and the Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine and Outer Shrine are the same deity. In the past, sake was brewed in the Misakado and offered before the gods. However, since brewing began to be conducted at the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|qq=}}, the Misakado is now used to temporarily store sake offered during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as during the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] in October.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 56, 58Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 36, 115 Four types of sake are offered at the Misakado: {{ill|shiroki|ja|白酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shiroki|qq=}} (white sake), {{ill|kuroki|ja|黒酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuroki|qq=}} (black sake), {{ill|reishu|ja|醴酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reishu|qq=}} (sweet sake), and {{ill|seishu|ja|清酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seishu|qq=}} (refined sake). Refined sake is donated by patrons, while the other three types are brewed at the Imibiya-den of the Inner Shrine.Sakurai (1970): p. 88 Additionally, during other rituals, reishu and seishu are offered, and for the {{ill|Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|ja|日別朝夕大御饌祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|qq=}}, only seishu is offered at the Misakado. In ancient times, the Misakado brewed two types of sake: '''Honashi Kiyosake''' (火無浄酒), made simply by adding water drawn from the sacred well of {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} to crushed rice called {{ill|shitogi|ja|粢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shitogi|qq=}}, and '''Homuke no Kanmiki''' (火向御酒), a type of [[:en:Amazake|amazake]].Sakurai (1970): p. 87 === Misakado Festival === The '''Misakado Festival''' (御酒殿祭) is held three times a year in June, October, and December. The festival prays for the successful brewing of sake offered to the gods and for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry, with {{ill|kōji|ja|糀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kōji|qq=}} (rice malt) being offered to the Misakado. During the festival held on September 25, 2013 (Heisei 25), 38 sake breweries from the Mie Prefecture Sake Brewers Association offered 38 4-to barrels of sake to the Inner Shrine.Yoshiko Kawaharada, "High-Quality Sake Offered for Prosperity of Brewing Industry," *Chunichi Shimbun*, September 26, 2013, Mie Edition, p. 20 == Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Misakado-no-Kami, under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine, is located to the left of the {{ill|Yuki-no-Mikura|ja|由貴御倉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Mikura|qq=}}, north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=}} in the Inner Shrine precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115 It is Ranked 5th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15–16 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16 Until the early modern period, white sake was brewed at the Imibiya-den by the {{ill|Sakatoku Monoimi|ja|酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatoku Monoimi|qq=}} and black sake was brewed at the purification site by the {{ill|Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|ja|清酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|qq=}}.Sakurai (1970): pp. 155–156 Black sake was brewed using water from the clear stream of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]].Sakurai (1970): p. 156 Since the Meiji era, priests have been responsible for brewing both white and black sake.Katō (1979): p. 284 According to the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Inner Shrine measured 40 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approximately 12 meters) in length, 17 shaku (approximately 5.1 meters) in width, and 8 shaku (approximately 2.4 meters) in height.Katō (1982): p. 807 == Misakado of the Outer Shrine == The Misakado of the Outer Shrine is located behind the Imibiya-den along the {{ill|Kita-gomon Sando|ja|北御門参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-gomon Sando|qq=}} within the Outer Shrine precinct. Since it is in a restricted area inaccessible to the general public, it is worshipped remotely from the front of the Imibiya-den.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 58 It Ranks first among the four shrines under the jurisdiction of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15, 17 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17 Although the Misakado of the Outer Shrine now stands quietly, until the early modern period, there was an administrative office next to it, along with sacred halls and three kitchens, where {{ill|naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naorai|qq=}} (sacred feasts) were held in the front courtyard. It was an important site for rituals of the Outer Shrine.Sakurai (1970): pp. 88–90 According to the ''Toyuke Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Outer Shrine measured 25 shaku (approximately 7.5 meters) in length, 16 shaku (approximately 4.8 meters) in width, and 9 shaku (approximately 2.7 meters) in height. == Sake Brewing at the Shrine == According to a paper by Hyakuichi Katō, the sake brewed for the 1977 (Shōwa 52) Kannamesai was prepared using 25 kg of white rice harvested from the sacred fields in Kusube-chō, Ise City, 2.7 kg of rice malt purchased from a supplier in [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], and 30 liters of water from the sacred well of Kami-no-Mii Shrine. It was brewed as {{ill|doburoku|ja|どぶろく|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/doburoku|qq=}} (unrefined sake) without a starter mash. Twelve days after brewing, the sake was divided into two: the plain batch became white sake, while ash from plants was added to create black sake. The total amount of sake brewed in 1977 was 180 liters. Although white and black sake are technically cloudy sake, they are classified as "refined sake" under Japanese law due to filtration. Reishu contains less than 1% alcohol, so it is not legally considered "sake." == Access == ; Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * Approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}}. Get off at ''Naikū-mae''.Mie Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Travel Guide]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Omikemichi) from Ise-nishi IC on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] or via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from Ise IC. Parking available nearby. ; Misakado of the Outer Shrine * Approximately a 5-minute walk from the south exit of Ise-shi Station (JR side) via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from the ''Ise-shi Ekimae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * Directly in front of the ''Gekū-mae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * About 5 minutes north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway. Parking for up to 448 vehicles is available nearby.Jingū Administration, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 加藤百一（1979）"清酒を造る神社"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''74'''(5):282-289. * 加藤百一（1982）"酒神と神社（12）"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''77'''(11):806-810. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 200px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area08/area08_14.jpg 御酒殿神（皇大神宮所管社）] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_25.jpg 御酒殿（豊受大神宮所管社）] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0116.html 御酒殿神（みさかどののかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0207.html 御酒殿（みさかどの） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御酒殿神|version=102708708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sake brewing in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御酒殿神]] [[sn:Misakadono-no-Kami]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naiku Misakadono-no-Kami 2.jpg|250px]]
Naikū-administered shrine: Misakadono-no-Kami | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} {{Shrine | Name = Misakadono | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Misakadononokami-geku.jpg|250px]]
Gekū-administered shrine: Misakadono | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} [[File:Geku Imibiyaden.jpg|thumb|Imibiyaden of Gekū
Misakadono is enshrined beyond this hall, where general visitors are not allowed to enter.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 36.]] '''Misakadono-no-Kami''' (御酒殿神) refers to both the deity and the [[Shinto shrine]] enshrining this deity located in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan. There are two such shrines: one is administered by {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū), and the other by {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū). During the '''Misakadono Festival''', prayers are offered not only for the safe brewing of sacred sake (神酒) at the shrine but also for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry.{{Cite web|lang=ja|author=Radio Cube FM Mie|authorlink=Mie FM Broadcasting|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|title=December 7 Broadcast: Misakadono|date=2013-04-04|accessdate=2013-09-14|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0912-2300-12/www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|archivedate=2013-09-12}}. == Overview == [[File:Sake produced in Mie prefecture.jpg|thumb|Sake from Mie Prefecture offered to the Inner Shrine]] This section describes information common to both shrines. The enshrined deity, Misakado-no-Kami, is the god of the {{ill|Misakado|ja|御酒殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakado|qq=}} (Sake Hall), and the Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine and Outer Shrine are the same deity. In the past, sake was brewed in the Misakado and offered before the gods. However, since brewing began to be conducted at the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|qq=}}, the Misakado is now used to temporarily store sake offered during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as during the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] in October.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 56, 58Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 36, 115 Four types of sake are offered at the Misakado: {{ill|shiroki|ja|白酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shiroki|qq=}} (white sake), {{ill|kuroki|ja|黒酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuroki|qq=}} (black sake), {{ill|reishu|ja|醴酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reishu|qq=}} (sweet sake), and {{ill|seishu|ja|清酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seishu|qq=}} (refined sake). Refined sake is donated by patrons, while the other three types are brewed at the Imibiya-den of the Inner Shrine.Sakurai (1970): p. 88 Additionally, during other rituals, reishu and seishu are offered, and for the {{ill|Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|ja|日別朝夕大御饌祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|qq=}}, only seishu is offered at the Misakado. In ancient times, the Misakado brewed two types of sake: '''Honashi Kiyosake''' (火無浄酒), made simply by adding water drawn from the sacred well of {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} to crushed rice called {{ill|shitogi|ja|粢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shitogi|qq=}}, and '''Homuke no Kanmiki''' (火向御酒), a type of [[:en:Amazake|amazake]].Sakurai (1970): p. 87 === Misakado Festival === The '''Misakado Festival''' (御酒殿祭) is held three times a year in June, October, and December. The festival prays for the successful brewing of sake offered to the gods and for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry, with {{ill|kōji|ja|糀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kōji|qq=}} (rice malt) being offered to the Misakado. During the festival held on September 25, 2013 (Heisei 25), 38 sake breweries from the Mie Prefecture Sake Brewers Association offered 38 4-to barrels of sake to the Inner Shrine.Yoshiko Kawaharada, "High-Quality Sake Offered for Prosperity of Brewing Industry," *Chunichi Shimbun*, September 26, 2013, Mie Edition, p. 20 == Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Misakado-no-Kami, under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine, is located to the left of the {{ill|Yuki-no-Mikura|ja|由貴御倉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Mikura|qq=}}, north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=}} in the Inner Shrine precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115 It is Ranked 5th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15–16 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16 Until the early modern period, white sake was brewed at the Imibiya-den by the {{ill|Sakatoku Monoimi|ja|酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatoku Monoimi|qq=}} and black sake was brewed at the purification site by the {{ill|Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|ja|清酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|qq=}}.Sakurai (1970): pp. 155–156 Black sake was brewed using water from the clear stream of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]].Sakurai (1970): p. 156 Since the Meiji era, priests have been responsible for brewing both white and black sake.Katō (1979): p. 284 According to the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Inner Shrine measured 40 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approximately 12 meters) in length, 17 shaku (approximately 5.1 meters) in width, and 8 shaku (approximately 2.4 meters) in height.Katō (1982): p. 807 == Misakado of the Outer Shrine == The Misakado of the Outer Shrine is located behind the Imibiya-den along the {{ill|Kita-gomon Sando|ja|北御門参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-gomon Sando|qq=}} within the Outer Shrine precinct. Since it is in a restricted area inaccessible to the general public, it is worshipped remotely from the front of the Imibiya-den.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 58 It Ranks first among the four shrines under the jurisdiction of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15, 17 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17 Although the Misakado of the Outer Shrine now stands quietly, until the early modern period, there was an administrative office next to it, along with sacred halls and three kitchens, where {{ill|naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naorai|qq=}} (sacred feasts) were held in the front courtyard. It was an important site for rituals of the Outer Shrine.Sakurai (1970): pp. 88–90 According to the ''Toyuke Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Outer Shrine measured 25 shaku (approximately 7.5 meters) in length, 16 shaku (approximately 4.8 meters) in width, and 9 shaku (approximately 2.7 meters) in height. == Sake Brewing at the Shrine == According to a paper by Hyakuichi Katō, the sake brewed for the 1977 (Shōwa 52) Kannamesai was prepared using 25 kg of white rice harvested from the sacred fields in Kusube-chō, Ise City, 2.7 kg of rice malt purchased from a supplier in [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], and 30 liters of water from the sacred well of Kami-no-Mii Shrine. It was brewed as {{ill|doburoku|ja|どぶろく|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/doburoku|qq=}} (unrefined sake) without a starter mash. Twelve days after brewing, the sake was divided into two: the plain batch became white sake, while ash from plants was added to create black sake. The total amount of sake brewed in 1977 was 180 liters. Although white and black sake are technically cloudy sake, they are classified as "refined sake" under Japanese law due to filtration. Reishu contains less than 1% alcohol, so it is not legally considered "sake." == Access == ; Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * Approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}}. Get off at ''Naikū-mae''.Mie Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Travel Guide]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Omikemichi) from Ise-nishi IC on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] or via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from Ise IC. Parking available nearby. ; Misakado of the Outer Shrine * Approximately a 5-minute walk from the south exit of Ise-shi Station (JR side) via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from the ''Ise-shi Ekimae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * Directly in front of the ''Gekū-mae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * About 5 minutes north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway. Parking for up to 448 vehicles is available nearby.Jingū Administration, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 加藤百一（1979）"清酒を造る神社"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''74'''(5):282-289. * 加藤百一（1982）"酒神と神社（12）"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''77'''(11):806-810. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 200px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area08/area08_14.jpg 御酒殿神（皇大神宮所管社）] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_25.jpg 御酒殿（豊受大神宮所管社）] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0116.html 御酒殿神（みさかどののかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0207.html 御酒殿（みさかどの） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御酒殿神|version=102708708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sake brewing in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御酒殿神]] [[sn:Misakadono-no-Kami]] [[Category:Alcohol deities]]

However, since brewing began to be conducted at the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|qq=}}, the Misakado is now used to temporarily store sake offered during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as during the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] in October.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 56, 58Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 36, 115 Four types of sake are offered at the Misakado: {{ill|shiroki|ja|白酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shiroki|qq=}} (white sake), {{ill|kuroki|ja|黒酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuroki|qq=}} (black sake), {{ill|reishu|ja|醴酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reishu|qq=}} (sweet sake), and {{ill|seishu|ja|清酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seishu|qq=}} (refined sake). Refined sake is donated by patrons, while the other three types are brewed at the Imibiya-den of the Inner Shrine.Sakurai (1970): p. 88 Additionally, during other rituals, reishu and seishu are offered, and for the {{ill|Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|ja|日別朝夕大御饌祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|qq=}}, only seishu is offered at the Misakado. In ancient times, the Misakado brewed two types of sake: '''Honashi Kiyosake''' (火無浄酒), made simply by adding water drawn from the sacred well of {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} to crushed rice called {{ill|shitogi|ja|粢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shitogi|qq=}}, and '''Homuke no Kanmiki''' (火向御酒), a type of [[:en:Amazake|amazake]].Sakurai (1970): p. 87 === Misakado Festival === The '''Misakado Festival''' (御酒殿祭) is held three times a year in June, October, and December. The festival prays for the successful brewing of sake offered to the gods and for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry, with {{ill|kōji|ja|糀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kōji|qq=}} (rice malt) being offered to the Misakado. During the festival held on September 25, 2013 (Heisei 25), 38 sake breweries from the Mie Prefecture Sake Brewers Association offered 38 4-to barrels of sake to the Inner Shrine.Yoshiko Kawaharada, "High-Quality Sake Offered for Prosperity of Brewing Industry," *Chunichi Shimbun*, September 26, 2013, Mie Edition, p. 20 == Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Misakado-no-Kami, under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine, is located to the left of the {{ill|Yuki-no-Mikura|ja|由貴御倉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Mikura|qq=}}, north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=}} in the Inner Shrine precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115 It is Ranked 5th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15–16 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16 Until the early modern period, white sake was brewed at the Imibiya-den by the {{ill|Sakatoku Monoimi|ja|酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatoku Monoimi|qq=}} and black sake was brewed at the purification site by the {{ill|Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|ja|清酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|qq=}}.Sakurai (1970): pp. 155–156 Black sake was brewed using water from the clear stream of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]].Sakurai (1970): p. 156 Since the Meiji era, priests have been responsible for brewing both white and black sake.Katō (1979): p. 284 According to the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Inner Shrine measured 40 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approximately 12 meters) in length, 17 shaku (approximately 5.1 meters) in width, and 8 shaku (approximately 2.4 meters) in height.Katō (1982): p. 807 == Misakado of the Outer Shrine == The Misakado of the Outer Shrine is located behind the Imibiya-den along the {{ill|Kita-gomon Sando|ja|北御門参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-gomon Sando|qq=}} within the Outer Shrine precinct. Since it is in a restricted area inaccessible to the general public, it is worshipped remotely from the front of the Imibiya-den.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 58 It Ranks first among the four shrines under the jurisdiction of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15, 17 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17 Although the Misakado of the Outer Shrine now stands quietly, until the early modern period, there was an administrative office next to it, along with sacred halls and three kitchens, where {{ill|naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naorai|qq=}} (sacred feasts) were held in the front courtyard. It was an important site for rituals of the Outer Shrine.Sakurai (1970): pp. 88–90 According to the ''Toyuke Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Outer Shrine measured 25 shaku (approximately 7.5 meters) in length, 16 shaku (approximately 4.8 meters) in width, and 9 shaku (approximately 2.7 meters) in height. == Sake Brewing at the Shrine == According to a paper by Hyakuichi Katō, the sake brewed for the 1977 (Shōwa 52) Kannamesai was prepared using 25 kg of white rice harvested from the sacred fields in Kusube-chō, Ise City, 2.7 kg of rice malt purchased from a supplier in [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], and 30 liters of water from the sacred well of Kami-no-Mii Shrine. It was brewed as {{ill|doburoku|ja|どぶろく|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/doburoku|qq=}} (unrefined sake) without a starter mash. Twelve days after brewing, the sake was divided into two: the plain batch became white sake, while ash from plants was added to create black sake. The total amount of sake brewed in 1977 was 180 liters. Although white and black sake are technically cloudy sake, they are classified as "refined sake" under Japanese law due to filtration. Reishu contains less than 1% alcohol, so it is not legally considered "sake." == Access == ; Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * Approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}}. Get off at ''Naikū-mae''.Mie Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Travel Guide]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Omikemichi) from Ise-nishi IC on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] or via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from Ise IC. Parking available nearby. ; Misakado of the Outer Shrine * Approximately a 5-minute walk from the south exit of Ise-shi Station (JR side) via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from the ''Ise-shi Ekimae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * Directly in front of the ''Gekū-mae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * About 5 minutes north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway. Parking for up to 448 vehicles is available nearby.Jingū Administration, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 加藤百一（1979）"清酒を造る神社"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''74'''(5):282-289. * 加藤百一（1982）"酒神と神社（12）"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''77'''(11):806-810. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 200px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area08/area08_14.jpg 御酒殿神（皇大神宮所管社）] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_25.jpg 御酒殿（豊受大神宮所管社）] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0116.html 御酒殿神（みさかどののかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0207.html 御酒殿（みさかどの） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御酒殿神|version=102708708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sake brewing in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御酒殿神]] [[sn:Misakadono-no-Kami]] [[Category:Alcohol deities]]

{{Shrine | Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naiku Misakadono-no-Kami 2.jpg|250px]]
Naikū-administered shrine: Misakadono-no-Kami | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} {{Shrine | Name = Misakadono | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Misakadononokami-geku.jpg|250px]]
Gekū-administered shrine: Misakadono | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} [[File:Geku Imibiyaden.jpg|thumb|Imibiyaden of Gekū
Misakadono is enshrined beyond this hall, where general visitors are not allowed to enter.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 36.]] '''Misakadono-no-Kami''' (御酒殿神) refers to both the deity and the [[Shinto shrine]] enshrining this deity located in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan. There are two such shrines: one is administered by {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū), and the other by {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū). During the '''Misakadono Festival''', prayers are offered not only for the safe brewing of sacred sake (神酒) at the shrine but also for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry.{{Cite web|lang=ja|author=Radio Cube FM Mie|authorlink=Mie FM Broadcasting|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|title=December 7 Broadcast: Misakadono|date=2013-04-04|accessdate=2013-09-14|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0912-2300-12/www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|archivedate=2013-09-12}}. == Overview == [[File:Sake produced in Mie prefecture.jpg|thumb|Sake from Mie Prefecture offered to the Inner Shrine]] This section describes information common to both shrines. The enshrined deity, Misakado-no-Kami, is the god of the {{ill|Misakado|ja|御酒殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御酒殿神}} (Sake Hall), and the Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine and Outer Shrine are the same deity. In the past, sake was brewed in the Misakado and offered before the gods. However, since brewing began to be conducted at the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Misakado is now used to temporarily store sake offered during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as during the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] in October.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 56, 58Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 36, 115 Four types of sake are offered at the Misakado: {{ill|shiroki|ja|白酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shiroki|qq=}} (white sake), {{ill|kuroki|ja|黒酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuroki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神酒}} (black sake), {{ill|reishu|ja|醴酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reishu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甘酒}} (sweet sake), and {{ill|seishu|ja|清酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seishu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本酒}} (refined sake). Refined sake is donated by patrons, while the other three types are brewed at the Imibiya-den of the Inner Shrine.Sakurai (1970): p. 88 Additionally, during other rituals, reishu and seishu are offered, and for the {{ill|Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|ja|日別朝夕大御饌祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, only seishu is offered at the Misakado. In ancient times, the Misakado brewed two types of sake: '''Honashi Kiyosake''' (火無浄酒), made simply by adding water drawn from the sacred well of {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} to crushed rice called {{ill|shitogi|ja|粢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shitogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米}}, and '''Homuke no Kanmiki''' (火向御酒), a type of [[:en:Amazake|amazake]].Sakurai (1970): p. 87 === Misakado Festival === The '''Misakado Festival''' (御酒殿祭) is held three times a year in June, October, and December. The festival prays for the successful brewing of sake offered to the gods and for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry, with {{ill|kōji|ja|糀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麹}} (rice malt) being offered to the Misakado. During the festival held on September 25, 2013 (Heisei 25), 38 sake breweries from the Mie Prefecture Sake Brewers Association offered 38 4-to barrels of sake to the Inner Shrine.Yoshiko Kawaharada, "High-Quality Sake Offered for Prosperity of Brewing Industry," *Chunichi Shimbun*, September 26, 2013, Mie Edition, p. 20 == Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Misakado-no-Kami, under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine, is located to the left of the {{ill|Yuki-no-Mikura|ja|由貴御倉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Mikura|qq=}}, north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Inner Shrine precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115 It is Ranked 5th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15–16 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16 Until the early modern period, white sake was brewed at the Imibiya-den by the {{ill|Sakatoku Monoimi|ja|酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatoku Monoimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and black sake was brewed at the purification site by the {{ill|Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|ja|清酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Sakurai (1970): pp. 155–156 Black sake was brewed using water from the clear stream of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]].Sakurai (1970): p. 156 Since the Meiji era, priests have been responsible for brewing both white and black sake.Katō (1979): p. 284 According to the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Inner Shrine measured 40 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approximately 12 meters) in length, 17 shaku (approximately 5.1 meters) in width, and 8 shaku (approximately 2.4 meters) in height.Katō (1982): p. 807 == Misakado of the Outer Shrine == The Misakado of the Outer Shrine is located behind the Imibiya-den along the {{ill|Kita-gomon Sando|ja|北御門参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-gomon Sando|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the Outer Shrine precinct. Since it is in a restricted area inaccessible to the general public, it is worshipped remotely from the front of the Imibiya-den.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 58 It Ranks first among the four shrines under the jurisdiction of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15, 17 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17 Although the Misakado of the Outer Shrine now stands quietly, until the early modern period, there was an administrative office next to it, along with sacred halls and three kitchens, where {{ill|naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naorai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (sacred feasts) were held in the front courtyard. It was an important site for rituals of the Outer Shrine.Sakurai (1970): pp. 88–90 According to the ''Toyuke Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Outer Shrine measured 25 shaku (approximately 7.5 meters) in length, 16 shaku (approximately 4.8 meters) in width, and 9 shaku (approximately 2.7 meters) in height. == Sake Brewing at the Shrine == According to a paper by Hyakuichi Katō, the sake brewed for the 1977 (Shōwa 52) Kannamesai was prepared using 25 kg of white rice harvested from the sacred fields in Kusube-chō, Ise City, 2.7 kg of rice malt purchased from a supplier in [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], and 30 liters of water from the sacred well of Kami-no-Mii Shrine. It was brewed as {{ill|doburoku|ja|どぶろく|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/doburoku|qq=}} (unrefined sake) without a starter mash. Twelve days after brewing, the sake was divided into two: the plain batch became white sake, while ash from plants was added to create black sake. The total amount of sake brewed in 1977 was 180 liters. Although white and black sake are technically cloudy sake, they are classified as "refined sake" under Japanese law due to filtration. Reishu contains less than 1% alcohol, so it is not legally considered "sake." == Access == ; Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * Approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at ''Naikū-mae''.Mie Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Travel Guide]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Omikemichi) from Ise-nishi IC on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] or via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from Ise IC. Parking available nearby. ; Misakado of the Outer Shrine * Approximately a 5-minute walk from the south exit of Ise-shi Station (JR side) via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from the ''Ise-shi Ekimae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * Directly in front of the ''Gekū-mae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * About 5 minutes north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway. Parking for up to 448 vehicles is available nearby.Jingū Administration, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 加藤百一（1979）"清酒を造る神社"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''74'''(5):282-289. * 加藤百一（1982）"酒神と神社（12）"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''77'''(11):806-810. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 200px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area08/area08_14.jpg 御酒殿神（皇大神宮所管社）] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_25.jpg 御酒殿（豊受大神宮所管社）] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0116.html 御酒殿神（みさかどののかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0207.html 御酒殿（みさかどの） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御酒殿神|version=102708708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Sake brewing in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御酒殿神]] [[sn:Misakadono-no-Kami]] [[Category:Alcohol deities]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naiku Misakadono-no-Kami 2.jpg|250px]]
Naikū-administered shrine: Misakadono-no-Kami | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} {{Shrine | Name = Misakadono | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Misakadononokami-geku.jpg|250px]]
Gekū-administered shrine: Misakadono | location = [[Ise, Mie]], [[Japan]] | deity = Misakadono-no-Kami | type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Name = Misakadono-no-Kami | Festivals = | Rituals = Misakadono Festival (held three times annually) }} [[File:Geku Imibiyaden.jpg|thumb|Imibiyaden of Gekū
Misakadono is enshrined beyond this hall, where general visitors are not allowed to enter.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 36.]] '''Misakadono-no-Kami''' (御酒殿神) refers to both the deity and the [[Shinto shrine]] enshrining this deity located in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan. There are two such shrines: one is administered by {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū), and the other by {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū). During the '''Misakadono Festival''', prayers are offered not only for the safe brewing of sacred sake (神酒) at the shrine but also for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry.{{Cite web|lang=ja|author=Radio Cube FM Mie|authorlink=Mie FM Broadcasting|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|title=December 7 Broadcast: Misakadono|date=2013-04-04|accessdate=2013-09-14|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0912-2300-12/www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=65|archivedate=2013-09-12}}. == Overview == [[File:Sake produced in Mie prefecture.jpg|thumb|Sake from Mie Prefecture offered to the Inner Shrine]] This section describes information common to both shrines. The enshrined deity, Misakado-no-Kami, is the god of the {{ill|Misakado|ja|御酒殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御酒殿神}} (Sake Hall), and the Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine and Outer Shrine are the same deity. In the past, sake was brewed in the Misakado and offered before the gods. However, since brewing began to be conducted at the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Misakado is now used to temporarily store sake offered during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as during the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] in October.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 56, 58Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 36, 115 Four types of sake are offered at the Misakado: {{ill|shiroki|ja|白酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shiroki|qq=}} (white sake), {{ill|kuroki|ja|黒酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuroki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神酒}} (black sake), {{ill|reishu|ja|醴酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reishu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甘酒}} (sweet sake), and {{ill|seishu|ja|清酒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seishu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本酒}} (refined sake). Refined sake is donated by patrons, while the other three types are brewed at the Imibiya-den of the Inner Shrine.Sakurai (1970): p. 88 Additionally, during other rituals, reishu and seishu are offered, and for the {{ill|Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|ja|日別朝夕大御饌祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higoto Asayu Ōmiketsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, only seishu is offered at the Misakado. In ancient times, the Misakado brewed two types of sake: '''Honashi Kiyosake''' (火無浄酒), made simply by adding water drawn from the sacred well of {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} to crushed rice called {{ill|shitogi|ja|粢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shitogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米}}, and '''Homuke no Kanmiki''' (火向御酒), a type of [[:en:Amazake|amazake]].Sakurai (1970): p. 87 === Misakado Festival === The '''Misakado Festival''' (御酒殿祭) is held three times a year in June, October, and December. The festival prays for the successful brewing of sake offered to the gods and for the prosperity of the Japanese sake industry, with {{ill|kōji|ja|糀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麹}} (rice malt) being offered to the Misakado. During the festival held on September 25, 2013 (Heisei 25), 38 sake breweries from the Mie Prefecture Sake Brewers Association offered 38 4-to barrels of sake to the Inner Shrine.Yoshiko Kawaharada, "High-Quality Sake Offered for Prosperity of Brewing Industry," *Chunichi Shimbun*, September 26, 2013, Mie Edition, p. 20 == Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Misakado-no-Kami, under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine, is located to the left of the {{ill|Yuki-no-Mikura|ja|由貴御倉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Mikura|qq=}}, north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Inner Shrine precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115 It is Ranked 5th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15–16 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16 Until the early modern period, white sake was brewed at the Imibiya-den by the {{ill|Sakatoku Monoimi|ja|酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatoku Monoimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and black sake was brewed at the purification site by the {{ill|Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|ja|清酒作物忌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosake Tsukuri Monoimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Sakurai (1970): pp. 155–156 Black sake was brewed using water from the clear stream of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]].Sakurai (1970): p. 156 Since the Meiji era, priests have been responsible for brewing both white and black sake.Katō (1979): p. 284 According to the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Inner Shrine measured 40 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approximately 12 meters) in length, 17 shaku (approximately 5.1 meters) in width, and 8 shaku (approximately 2.4 meters) in height.Katō (1982): p. 807 == Misakado of the Outer Shrine == The Misakado of the Outer Shrine is located behind the Imibiya-den along the {{ill|Kita-gomon Sando|ja|北御門参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-gomon Sando|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the Outer Shrine precinct. Since it is in a restricted area inaccessible to the general public, it is worshipped remotely from the front of the Imibiya-den.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 58 It Ranks first among the four shrines under the jurisdiction of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 15, 17 The shrine was rebuilt in November 1910 (Meiji 43).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17 Although the Misakado of the Outer Shrine now stands quietly, until the early modern period, there was an administrative office next to it, along with sacred halls and three kitchens, where {{ill|naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naorai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (sacred feasts) were held in the front courtyard. It was an important site for rituals of the Outer Shrine.Sakurai (1970): pp. 88–90 According to the ''Toyuke Jingū Gishiki-chō,'' the Misakado of the Outer Shrine measured 25 shaku (approximately 7.5 meters) in length, 16 shaku (approximately 4.8 meters) in width, and 9 shaku (approximately 2.7 meters) in height. == Sake Brewing at the Shrine == According to a paper by Hyakuichi Katō, the sake brewed for the 1977 (Shōwa 52) Kannamesai was prepared using 25 kg of white rice harvested from the sacred fields in Kusube-chō, Ise City, 2.7 kg of rice malt purchased from a supplier in [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], and 30 liters of water from the sacred well of Kami-no-Mii Shrine. It was brewed as {{ill|doburoku|ja|どぶろく|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/doburoku|qq=}} (unrefined sake) without a starter mash. Twelve days after brewing, the sake was divided into two: the plain batch became white sake, while ash from plants was added to create black sake. The total amount of sake brewed in 1977 was 180 liters. Although white and black sake are technically cloudy sake, they are classified as "refined sake" under Japanese law due to filtration. Reishu contains less than 1% alcohol, so it is not legally considered "sake." == Access == ; Misakado-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * Approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at ''Naikū-mae''.Mie Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Travel Guide]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Omikemichi) from Ise-nishi IC on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] or via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from Ise IC. Parking available nearby. ; Misakado of the Outer Shrine * Approximately a 5-minute walk from the south exit of Ise-shi Station (JR side) via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from the ''Ise-shi Ekimae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * Directly in front of the ''Gekū-mae'' bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * About 5 minutes north via Mie Prefectural Route 32 from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway. Parking for up to 448 vehicles is available nearby.Jingū Administration, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 12, 2013) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 加藤百一（1979）"清酒を造る神社"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''74'''(5):282-289. * 加藤百一（1982）"酒神と神社（12）"日本釀造協會雜誌（日本醸造協会）.'''77'''(11):806-810. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 200px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area08/area08_14.jpg 御酒殿神（皇大神宮所管社）] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_25.jpg 御酒殿（豊受大神宮所管社）] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0116.html 御酒殿神（みさかどののかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0207.html 御酒殿（みさかどの） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御酒殿神|version=102708708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Sake brewing in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御酒殿神]] [[sn:Misakadono-no-Kami]] [[Category:Alcohol deities]]
